contaminant.name: Carbon Deposit Buildup
contaminant.description: Carbon-buildup manifests as hard deposits from combustion and pyrolysis processes, which form tenacious layers on surfaces exposed to high-heat environments. This contamination, it distinguishes itself from softer residues by its dense, adherent structure that resists mechanical abrasion, particularly on steel and stainless steel where it embeds deeply, while on alumina or silicon carbide it appears more superficial yet persistent. Removal challenges arise from its thermal stability, influenced from substrate variations, but laser cleaning proves effective, as the beam vaporizes the buildup selectively without altering the underlying materials.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Carbon Monoxide, Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs), Ultrafine Particles - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (10 ACH) for particulate control
